title: QA: BackgroundShellManager::spawn returns Result<_, String> (stringly-typed error)
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/tools/bash/mod.rs:54, 173, 230
## Violation
`spawn`, `get_output`, and `kill` all return `Result<_, String>`. Errors cannot be matched on programmatically; callers must `contains()`-substring-test error messages (and the tests at lines 506-520, 626-630 do exactly that). Changes to the error string silently break consumers.

Quoted (mod.rs:54, 173, 230):
```
pub(crate) fn spawn(&self, command: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
...
pub(crate) fn get_output(&self, shell_id: &str) -> Result<(String, bool, Option<i32>), String> {
...
pub(crate) fn kill(&self, shell_id: &str) -> Result<String, String> {
```
## Mandated refactor
1. Add `#[derive(thiserror::Error, Debug)] pub enum ShellError { Validation(String), CapacityReached, SpawnFailed(io::Error), ShellNotFound { id: String }, Poisoned }`.
2. Return `Result<_, ShellError>` from each method.
3. Move display-string formatting into the `Display` impl.
4. Update tests to match enum variants, not substrings.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
